title: Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: Macron pays homage to late scholar and Resistance fighter Marc Bloch, who joins Pantheon
250 years of American independence: The birth and original sin of a new world
Europe bets on digital euro to counter the overwhelming dominance of the US dollar
subhead: The European Parliament will examine on Tuesday the creation of a digital euro system, designed to make payments cheaper and faster and, above all, to reduce Europe's dependence on US intermediaries.

title: Grieving Deschamps to miss France's match against Norway
subhead: France head coach Didier Deschamps’ mother died on Tuesday, so he will be returning to France for the funeral, with Guy Stéphan taking over for Friday's final Group I game against Norway. The 2026 World Cup is Deschamps' final tournament in charge of France.

title: Ten years after Brexit, the forgotten temptation of 'Frexit'
subhead: While both supporters of far-right Marine Le Pen and radical-left Jean-Luc Mélenchon celebrated Brexit after June 23, 2016, referendum, they have since abandoned the idea of France leaving the EU.

title: Serving a husband, God and America: Young female conservatives gather at San Antonio's Turning Point summit
subhead: In early June, around 3,000 women attended the Women's Leadership Summit in San Antonio, Texas. This annual event brings together a variety of conservative figures and influencers, relying on the extensive network of Turning Point USA, founded by right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. After convincing a majority of young men to vote for Trump in 2024, the organization now targets female voters from Gen Z.

France's public pools need urgent renovation: 'What would we do without them? We wouldn't make it'
subhead: Aging, energy-intensive and expensive for rural towns to maintain, many aquatic centers in the countryside have been shutting down. Yet these facilities provide equal access for people to cool off and learn to swim. An MP recently sounded the alarm about the worrying situation facing these essential local public services.

title: France faces unprecedented heatwave: 'We are about to experience four of the hottest days ever recorded in the country'
subhead: A prolonged heatwave, rather than a brief spike, has arrived. Unusually high temperatures are set to persist across the country until at least the end of this week, exposing more than 90% of the French population to extreme and rare heat.

title: The (not so) stupid questions to ask during a heatwave in France
subhead: Can we swim in the Seine? Should I eat meat when it is 40 degrees? Can I ask for a glass of water in any café? Le Monde answers your questions.
